This model allows each aircraft type to have its own fatality rate while borrowing strength across types.

```{r}
#| label: model3-prep

# For the hierarchical model, we need aircraft types with multiple observations
# Let's see the distribution
aircraft_counts <- crashes %>%
  count(aircraft_type, sort = TRUE)

cat("Aircraft Type Distribution:\n")
cat("===========================\n")
cat(sprintf("Total unique aircraft types: %d\n", nrow(aircraft_counts)))
cat(sprintf("Aircraft types with >= 5 crashes: %d\n", sum(aircraft_counts$n >= 5)))
cat(sprintf("Aircraft types with >= 10 crashes: %d\n", sum(aircraft_counts$n >= 10)))

# Filter to aircraft types with at least 3 observations
min_crashes <- 3
data_hierarchical <- crashes %>%
  group_by(aircraft_type) %>%
  filter(n() >= min_crashes) %>%
  ungroup()

cat(sprintf("\nUsing aircraft types with >= %d crashes\n", min_crashes))
cat(sprintf("Filtered dataset: %d crashes, %d aircraft types\n", 
            nrow(data_hierarchical), 
            n_distinct(data_hierarchical$aircraft_type)))
```

```{r}
#| label: model3
#| cache: true

# Set a flag for quick testing vs final run
QUICK_TEST <- TRUE  # Set to TRUE during development

if (QUICK_TEST) {
  chains_n <- 2
  iter_n <- 1000
  warmup_n <- 500
  test_data <- data_hierarchical %>% sample_frac(0.3)
} else {
  chains_n <- 4
  iter_n <- 2000
  warmup_n <- 1000
  test_data <- data_hierarchical
}

# Specify hierarchical model
model3 <- brm(
  fatalities | trials(aboard) ~ year_scaled + (1 | aircraft_type),
  data = test_data,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  prior = c(
    prior(normal(0, 2), class = Intercept),
    prior(normal(0, 1), class = b),
    prior(exponential(1), class = sd)  # Prior on group-level SD
  ),
  chains = chains_n,
  iter = iter_n,
  warmup = warmup_n,
  cores = 4,
  seed = 123,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95)
)

# Model summary
summary(model3)
```

## Model 3 Diagnostics

```{r}
#| label: model3-diagnostics
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6

# Posterior predictive check
pp_check(model3, ndraws = 100, type = "dens_overlay") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Predictive Check: Model 2")

# Trace plots
mcmc_plot(model3, type = "trace", 
          variable = c("b_Intercept", "b_year_scaled", "sd_aircraft_type__Intercept"))
```

## Model 3 Results

```{r}
#| label: model3-results
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 10

# Extract random effects
ranef_model3 <- ranef(model3)

# Get top and bottom aircraft types
aircraft_effects <- ranef_model3$aircraft_type %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("aircraft_type") %>%
  arrange(Estimate.Intercept)

# Plot top 20 safest and 20 most dangerous aircraft
n_show <- 20
aircraft_to_plot <- bind_rows(
  head(aircraft_effects, n_show),
  tail(aircraft_effects, n_show)
)

aircraft_to_plot %>%
  mutate(aircraft_type = fct_reorder(aircraft_type, Estimate.Intercept)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Estimate.Intercept, y = aircraft_type)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Q2.5.Intercept, xmax = Q97.5.Intercept), height = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Aircraft-Specific Fatality Rate Deviations",
       subtitle = paste0("Top ", n_show, " safest and ", n_show, " most dangerous aircraft types"),
       x = "Deviation from Mean (logit scale)", 
       y = "Aircraft Type") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7))

# Compare fixed effects
posterior3 <- as_draws_df(model3)

posterior3 %>%
  select(b_Intercept, b_year_scaled) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "parameter", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = parameter)) +
  stat_halfeye() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Distributions: Model 2 (Fixed Effects)",
       x = "Parameter Value", y = "Parameter")
```

## Visualize Group-Level Variation

```{r}
#| label: model3-variation
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6

# Extract group-level SD
sd_aircraft <- posterior3 %>%
  select(sd_aircraft_type__Intercept) %>%
  pull()

# Plot distribution
data.frame(sd = sd_aircraft) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = sd)) +
  stat_halfeye() +
  labs(title = "Group-Level Standard Deviation",
       subtitle = "Variation in fatality rates across aircraft types",
       x = "Standard Deviation (logit scale)", y = "Density")

# Summary statistics
cat("Group-level SD:\n",
    sprintf("Mean: %.3f\n", mean(sd_aircraft)),
    sprintf("Median: %.3f\n", median(sd_aircraft)),
    sprintf("95%% CI: [%.3f, %.3f]\n", 
            quantile(sd_aircraft, 0.025), 
            quantile(sd_aircraft, 0.975)),
    sep = "")

# Interpret
baseline_rate <- plogis(0)
low_aircraft <- plogis(-2 * mean(sd_aircraft))
high_aircraft <- plogis(2 * mean(sd_aircraft))

cat(sprintf("\nImplied fatality rate range (±2 SD):\n"),
    sprintf("Baseline: %.1f%%\n", 100 * baseline_rate),
    sprintf("Safe aircraft (-2 SD): %.1f%%\n", 100 * low_aircraft),
    sprintf("Dangerous aircraft (+2 SD): %.1f%%\n", 100 * high_aircraft),
    sep = "")
```
